title: Debt Alarms Ring as Countries Rack Up More Emergency Spending
summary: Amid signs of a prolonged period of high energy costs, policymakers are urging restraint as governments open up the public till to protect households and businesses.

title: Stocks Approach Record High as Wall St. Looks Beyond War
summary: Investors appear to be treating an end to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran as a foregone conclusion.

title: Trump Breaks With Meloni, Italy’s Leader, Amid Dispute Over Pope and Iran
summary: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was once considered one of President Trump’s closest European allies. Their friendship now appears in danger.

title: Vance Says Pope Leo Should Be More Careful When Talking About Theology
summary: The vice president, who is Catholic, took issue with Pope Leo XIV’s statement that disciples of Christ are “never on the side of those who once wielded the sword and today drop bombs.”

title: Trump’s Go-To Justification for Contentious Decisions: National Security
summary: The administration has invoked national security in a variety of matters, including the White House ballroom and offshore wind farms, drawing rebukes from some judges.

title: Ad Companies Settle With F.T.C. Over Claims of Harm to Conservative Sites
summary: WPP, Dentsu and Publicis settled claims they colluded on policies to combat misinformation, denying ad revenue to publishers on the right.

title: Trump Increased Tax Refunds, but a Political Challenge Still Looms
summary: While last year’s tax law has raised the average I.R.S. refund, the boost is less than the White House expected — and may not have much impact on voters.

title: What Is ‘Jagged Intelligence’ and How Can It Reframe the AI Debate?
summary: A.I. has always been compared to human intelligence, but that may not be the right way to think about it. What it does well can help predict what jobs it may replace.

title: That Meeting You Hate May Keep A.I. From Stealing Your Job
summary: As artificial intelligence makes many tasks easier, the human work of cajoling, arm-twisting and reassuring appears to be rising in importance.

title: We Don’t Really Know How A.I. Works. That’s a Problem.
summary: For us to trust it on certain subjects, researchers in the growing field of interpretability might need to learn how to open the black box of its brain.

title: Sister Mary Kay and the Waning Days of the Sisters of Charity
summary: An influential order of nuns decided to complete its mission when the last sister dies. The only question left is how to finish well.

title: ‘We Cannot Lose Sight of Ukraine.’ Europeans Promise More Aid to Kyiv.
summary: The focus in recent weeks has been on the conflict in Iran, but European leaders said it was crucial to keep aid flowing to Ukraine.

title: Vivek Ramaswamy’s Ohio Governor Bid: Can He Win Over Voters?
summary: Vivek Ramaswamy has all but cleared the field ahead of the May primary for Ohio governor, but whether a finance and pharma billionaire is the man for the moment is another question.

title: A Progressive Group Rolls Out a Campus Competitor to Turning Point
summary: More Perfect Union, a left-wing media organization, hopes to win back young voters and build a new generation of college influencers with its More Perfect University program.

title: What the New Loan Caps Will Mean for Grad Students This Fall
summary: With new limits on federal lending, many students will need private loans and some could be shut out. See the data, program by program.

title: As Los Angeles Rebuilds, a Surge of Experimentation
summary: Across the city’s fire zones, there’s a surge of experimentation — collective rebuilding, catalog homes and new technologies that are safe and reduce costs.

title: SantaCon Leader Ran His Own $1 Million Con Game, U.S. Says
summary: Stefan Pildes “siphoned” more than half of the money donated over five years for the event, a raucous New York City bar crawl promoted as a charitable event, prosecutors said.

summary: Mark Rosenblatt’s Broadway play, starring John Lithgow as the British children’s book author, draws from Dahl’s comments over the years.

summary: In Mark Rosenblatt’s play, a powerful portrayal of the beloved children’s book author who almost gleefully exposes his bigotry.

summary: Your pet is (probably) not a genius, and that’s OK.

summary: Trump is in over his head.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum’s War on Crime in Mexico Faces a Grim Reckoning: 133,000 Missing People
summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum of Mexico is under pressure to end one of her country’s most painful tragedies: the disappearance of more than 133,000 people.

title: Cheboygan Dam Failure Could Imperil Thousands in Northern Michigan
summary: A levee breach near Cheboygan had forced evacuations and officials continue to monitor a nearby dam. Rain and snowmelt have affected the area.

title: Paris Man Wins $1.2 Million Picasso Painting in Charity Raffle
summary: A Parisian software salesman entered a charity raffle and came away with a piece of history: “I have some paintings, but not like a Picasso.”

title: Surgeon Who Removed Wrong Organ From Patient Is Charged in His Death
summary: Dr. Thomas Shaknovsky tried to persuade his colleagues in the operating room that the liver he removed from a 70-year-old patient was a spleen, according to Florida’s Health Department.
